data.body:
## Description

**What**: Enhance project-setup to auto-detect existing Jira environment variables and guide users through missing values with contextual help.
**Why**: Users with JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, or JIRA_API_TOKEN already in their environment should see these offered as defaults rather than typing them again. Users without them need clear guidance (one-line descriptions and relevant URLs) to find the right values.
**Scope**:
- IN: Auto-detect JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, JIRA_API_TOKEN from environment; display descriptions and URLs for missing values; user confirmation before copying JIRA_API_TOKEN to GitHub secret; jira.project configuration in dso-config.conf
- OUT: ACLI resolution (Story 2), bot identity (Story 4), gh fallback (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the installer checks the local environment for JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, and JIRA_API_TOKEN and offers found values as pre-filled defaults
  Satisfies: "The installer auto-detects existing Jira environment variables and offers them as defaults"
- When this story is complete, for values not found in the environment, the installer displays a one-line description and the relevant URL before prompting
  Satisfies: "the installer displays a one-line description of the value and the relevant URL"
- When this story is complete, the user is asked to confirm before JIRA_API_TOKEN is copied to a GitHub secret
  Satisfies: "The user confirms before any secret is copied to GitHub"
- When this story is complete, jira.project is written to dso-config.conf
  Satisfies: "jira.project are fully configured by project-setup"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Security] JIRA_API_TOKEN crosses trust boundary (local env to GitHub secret) — user must confirm
- [Testing] Needs mock-compatible interface for gh subprocess calls
